title: Text, Unicode, Formatting, and Parsing · Program Defense
summary: Normalize multilingual community-event records and generate readable confirmations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a multilingual event text normalizer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create string literals with correct quoting, escapes, raw forms, multiline content, and Unicode characters.
Use string indexing, slicing, membership, length, and immutability without off-by-one assumptions.
Choose search, split, join, strip, replace, case, and predicate methods from the actual text contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Python Regex Boundaries applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use regular expressions for justified pattern structure while preferring simpler string operations for simple parsing.
